script: I used to work the night shift at a small 24-hour convenience store on the edge of town, the kind of place that’s always quiet after midnight except for the occasional truck driver or someone grabbing snacks. Most nights were incredibly boring. I’d restock shelves, wipe counters, and watch the security cameras while waiting for the clock to crawl toward morning. Around 2 or 3 AM the world outside would get really still, like the entire town was asleep and the store was the only place left awake. One night during a particularly slow shift, a guy walked in wearing a hoodie and sunglasses, which already felt weird because it was the middle of the night. He didn’t say anything, just slowly walked down the aisles looking at things. I kept watching him on the mirror behind the counter because something about the way he moved felt off. He wasn’t really shopping, just picking things up, staring at them, and putting them back like he’d never seen normal products before. After about ten minutes he brought a single bottle of water to the counter and put it down without saying a word. I rang it up and told him the price, but he just stood there staring at the card reader like it was some kind of puzzle. Finally he tapped his card and the payment went through, but he didn’t leave right away. Instead he asked me a really strange question: “Does it always feel this quiet here?” I kind of laughed and told him yeah, nights are usually dead. He nodded slowly like that confirmed something important, grabbed the water, and walked out. The weird part is that he didn’t get into a car or anything. Through the front window I watched him walk across the empty parking lot and disappear behind the building. About twenty minutes later I was in the back room grabbing a box of snacks to restock when I heard the front door chime again. I came back out expecting another customer, but the store was completely empty. I checked the aisles, looked outside, and even checked the security monitor to see if someone had walked in and left quickly, but there was nothing. The door sensor had definitely gone off though. At that point I figured maybe the wind had pushed the door or something and went back to restocking. The shift continued normally for another hour until around 4 AM when the same guy walked back in. Same hoodie, same sunglasses, same quiet way of moving. He walked straight to the counter and put the same brand of water bottle down again. I joked and said, “Didn’t you already buy one earlier?” He paused for a second and then said, “Yeah… but I finished that timeline already.” I laughed because I thought he was joking, but he didn’t laugh back. He just paid, picked up the water, and left again. The moment he walked out, the automatic door closed behind him and the store went completely silent. I checked the cameras later out of curiosity and the weirdest part was that the recording from the moment the door chime rang earlier that night was just… missing. Like the footage skipped ahead by a few seconds. It’s probably just some boring technical glitch, but I still think about that guy sometimes and wonder what he meant by finishing a timeline.
